behavior_summary: This script implements Kilandra, an NPC with: an examine/"U8P_KILANDRA::look" description handler, proximity lifecycle (enter/leave fast area), ambient barks/monologues, a player conversation/menu handler, and reactions to assault/theft.

U8P_KILANDRA::look returns a short description when the player examines Kilandra, choosing among variants (dead body wording vs. named/job variants) based on Npc::isDead(this) and local global flags kilandraName and kilandraJob.

Proximity lifecycle: U8P_KILANDRA::enterFastArea clears interaction references and launches a nearby-behavior utility (U8P_KILANDRA::func0495) which attempts to pathfind toward a nearby target and, on success, triggers ambient dialog via U8P_KILANDRA::func05AC. U8P_KILANDRA::leaveFastArea clears refs and teleports the NPC to a random location if the NPC is dead.

Scheduling: U8P_KILANDRA::schedule picks per-instance U8P_KILANDRA::schedule/location bitmasks based on FREE::getTimeOfDayPeriod() and calls METHOD::func133F to apply them (inferred to set NPC routing/placement).

Dialog/interaction: U8P_KILANDRA::use builds a dynamic menu (local2) using MenuSet/MenuUnion/MenuAdd/MenuRemove, presents Ask to the player, captures the choice (local1), strcmp compares it and branches. It sets flags like kilandraMet, kilandraName and kilandraJob in response to player input and supports multi-step gating (askedFirstTime/askedSecondTime/gwillimToldDoor).

Crime reactions: U8P_KILANDRA::cast and U8P_KILANDRA::AvatarStoleSomething notify guards or escalate (CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime, METHOD::func091C), clear interaction refs, and bark alarm/accusatory lines. U8P_KILANDRA::AvatarStoleSomething also spawns follow-up timed behavior and calls U8P_KILANDRA::func0231 to continue theft handling.
notable_control_flow: Examine (U8P_KILANDRA::look): branch on Npc::isDead(this) first; then prefer 'name+job' -> 'name' -> 'job' -> generic descriptor based on kilandraName/kilandraJob flags (high confidence).

Proximity approach (U8P_KILANDRA::func0495): loop limited by a local attempt counter (local2) performing spawn Npc::pathfind and checking process_result() — on success call METHOD::func15E7 and U8P_KILANDRA::func05AC; on failure spawn FREE::func1C19 fallback and retry (medium confidence).

Conversation (U8P_KILANDRA::use): conversation loop with dynamic menu composition; Ask -> SuspendAssign(local1) -> strcmp(local1, option) dispatches branches; menu contents are gated by flags (kilandraMet, gwillimToldDoor, askedFirstTime, askedSecondTime, kilandraPissed, deadWalk) and flags are set as the player gives information (high confidence).

Crime handling: U8P_KILANDRA::cast() polls/tries a few times (local1 counter) then, if targeted and alive, calls guard-respond and barks; U8P_KILANDRA::AvatarStoleSomething records param and sequences a METHOD::func091C call, bark, spawn -> FREE::func1C19 and then calls U8P_KILANDRA::func0231 (medium confidence).
open_questions: What exact bits/meanings are encoded by the mask values set in U8P_KILANDRA::schedule (0x2DDF / 0x2C7F / 0x3EDF / 0x451F)? These appear to be location/U8P_KILANDRA::schedule bitmasks but their mapping to map locations or routines is unknown (low confidence).

What does U8P_KILANDRA::func0231 specifically do after U8P_KILANDRA::AvatarStoleSomething triggers it (pursuit, report, or dialog)? It is referenced but not present in the extracted helper list (low confidence).

Exact semantics of the integer parameter passed to U8P_KILANDRA::AvatarStoleSomething: is it an item id, theft context code, or stealing actor id? The code stores it but U8P_KILANDRA::use is unclear (medium confidence).

Are any global flags beyond the listed ones written by other game scripts that change Kilandra's available menu options (e.g., external quests influencing gwillimToldDoor)? Context is inferred but not fully enumerated (low confidence).

main_function.name: U8P_KILANDRA::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/description text for Kilandra, selecting wording depending on whether Kilandra is dead and on instance flags that record whether the NPC has a name or job label.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's 'U8P_KILANDRA::look' / examine action targeting this NPC instance.
Branches immediately on Npc::isDead(this) to choose dead vs. alive phrasing.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::look
function_map.role: Responds to player 'U8P_KILANDRA::look' action; returns short description/name variants based on dead/alive and kilandraName/kilandraJob flags.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::schedule
function_map.role: Set per-instance U8P_KILANDRA::schedule/location bitmasks according to FREE::getTimeOfDayPeriod() and call METHOD::func133F to apply routing/placement.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: clear interaction refs and invoke U8P_KILANDRA::func0495 to begin proximity behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: clear interaction refs; if Npc::isDead(this) then teleport this NPC to a random location.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::cast
function_map.role: Handle being targeted/assaulted: on target present and alive notify guards, clear refs and bark; includes a short retry/suspend loop when no target present.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Handle theft event: if alive clear refs, call METHOD::func091C, bark, spawn a follow-up timed process and call U8P_KILANDRA::func0231 for continued handling.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::func0495
function_map.role: Proximity utility: inspect nearby item frame/status, attempt repeated spawn Npc::pathfind calls and on success call METHOD::func15E7 then U8P_KILANDRA::func05AC; fallback to timed spawn when pathfind fails.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::func05AC
function_map.role: Ambient monologue/bark routine selecting among many short spoken variants with distance/status checks and randomized choice; used when the NPC is near the player.

function_map.name: U8P_KILANDRA::use
function_map.role: Conversation/menu handler: build dynamic menu options (local2), present Ask, capture choice (local1) and branch on strcmp; sets flags like kilandraMet/kilandraName/kilandraJob and supports multi-step gated reveals.

internal_calls.purpose: Start proximity approach/interaction behavior after clearing refs.
Invoke ambient dialog/monologue after a successful pathfind/approach.
Continue theft-handling behavior (pursuit, report, or further dialog); exact role is not present in the extracted functions and is inferred.
Indirectly related: same script helpers referenced in conversation flow and NPC lifecycle, U8P_KILANDRA::schedule used to set the NPC's routing state (inferred associations only).

external_calls.purpose: Read current time-of-day period to select U8P_KILANDRA::schedule/location bitmasks.
Apply/commit the chosen U8P_KILANDRA::schedule/location flags (inferred to place or route the NPC).
Clear interaction/process references (cleanup) before starting proximity behavior.
Teleport the NPC to a random X/Y when the NPC is dead (used on leaving fast area).
Request/notify guards when the NPC is assaulted (param1 == 1 and NPC alive).
Obtain current target to decide whether the NPC is being attacked/U8P_KILANDRA::cast on.
Higher-level theft/accusation routine invoked as part of theft response.
Spawn a pathfinding/move attempt toward a nearby target and inspect process_result() to detect success/failure.
Spawn a timed fallback process when pathfind fails (used to U8P_KILANDRA::schedule retries or cooldown).
Test player/NPC distance against thresholds to gate whether certain bark variants should play.
Check item/status bits that influence whether nearer-distance bark variants can run.
Retrieve player's name to build a personalized menu/dialog string (local3).

reads.name: kilandraName
reads.likely_effect: Used by U8P_KILANDRA::look to decide U8P_KILANDRA::use of the NPC's personal name in the description and by U8P_KILANDRA::use() for menu flow.

reads.name: kilandraJob
reads.likely_effect: Used by U8P_KILANDRA::look to include the NPC's job/title (fisherwoman) and by conversation gating.

reads.name: kilandraMet
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_KILANDRA::func05AC and U8P_KILANDRA::use to choose 'first met' vs. repeat dialog variants and initial greeting.

reads.name: gwillimToldDoor
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_KILANDRA::use to gate ghost/key-related menu options (dialog branching depends on this external information).

reads.name: askedFirstTime
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_KILANDRA::use to select which step of a multi-step info reveal to present to the player.

reads.name: askedSecondTime
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_KILANDRA::use alongside askedFirstTime to enable deeper follow-up menu options.

reads.name: kilandraPissed
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_KILANDRA::use to present an alternate anger menu and suppress normal dialog options when set.

reads.name: deadWalk
reads.likely_effect: Read by U8P_KILANDRA::use to adjust initial greeting wording when the NPC is in a dead/walking state.

writes.name: kilandraMet
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player first identifies themselves or interacts; toggles later greetings to 'met before' variants.

writes.name: kilandraName
writes.likely_effect: Set when the conversation records the NPC's personal name or when player provides a name; alters U8P_KILANDRA::look() wording and possibly dialog branches.

writes.name: kilandraJob
writes.likely_effect: Set when the player or script records the NPC's job (fisherwoman); used in U8P_KILANDRA::look() descriptions and menu gating.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements Kilandra, an NPC with: an examine/"U8P_KILANDRA::look" description handler, proximity lifecycle (enter/leave fast area), ambient barks/monologues, a player conversation/menu handler, and reactions to assault/theft.
